scenarioDescription: A company is migrating a legacy three-tier web application to AWS. The application needs a highly available architecture across multiple Availability Zones with automatic scaling for the web tier, a managed relational database with read replicas, and static assets served with low latency globally.
toolboxServices: Elastic Load Balancing (ALB)|Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling|Amazon RDS Multi-AZ|Amazon CloudFront|Amazon S3|Amazon DynamoDB|AWS Elastic Beanstalk
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon CloudFront|Elastic Load Balancing (ALB)|Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling|Amazon RDS Multi-AZ|Amazon S3
hint: Global users should hit a CDN first before reaching your load balancer.
hint2: Static assets like images and CSS should be stored in object storage and served through the CDN, not from your EC2 instances.
explanation: Amazon CloudFront serves as the global CDN entry point, caching static content from Amazon S3 and routing dynamic requests to the Elastic Load Balancing (ALB), which distributes traffic across Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling groups spanning multiple AZs. Amazon RDS Multi-AZ provides the managed relational database with automatic failover, while Amazon S3 stores static assets as the CloudFront origin. This architecture achieves high availability, automatic scaling, and low-latency global content delivery.

scenarioDescription: A startup needs to build a serverless REST API that authenticates users, processes business logic, stores data in a NoSQL database, and returns JSON responses. They want zero server management and pay-per-request pricing.
toolboxServices: Amazon API Gateway|AWS Lambda|Amazon DynamoDB|Amazon Cognito|Amazon RDS|Amazon EC2|Amazon ElastiCache
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon Cognito|Amazon API Gateway|AWS Lambda|Amazon DynamoDB
hint: User authentication should happen before the API request reaches your business logic.
hint2: A fully managed NoSQL database with on-demand capacity mode provides true pay-per-request pricing without provisioning.
explanation: Amazon Cognito authenticates users and issues JWT tokens that Amazon API Gateway validates using a Cognito authorizer before routing requests. AWS Lambda executes the business logic as serverless functions triggered by API Gateway, with no server management required. Amazon DynamoDB stores the data in on-demand capacity mode for true pay-per-request pricing, completing the fully serverless architecture with zero infrastructure management.

scenarioDescription: A media company needs to process video files uploaded by users. When a video is uploaded, it must be automatically transcoded into multiple formats (720p, 1080p, 4K), with the original and transcoded files stored durably. The system must handle unpredictable upload volumes without manual intervention.
toolboxServices: Amazon S3|AWS Lambda|Amazon SQS|AWS Elastic Transcoder|Amazon EFS|Amazon EC2|Amazon Kinesis
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon S3|AWS Lambda|Amazon SQS|AWS Elastic Transcoder|Amazon S3
hint: Object storage can trigger event notifications automatically when new files are uploaded.
hint2: A message queue between the trigger and the transcoding service decouples the components and handles burst uploads gracefully.
explanation: Users upload videos to Amazon S3, which triggers an AWS Lambda function via S3 event notifications. Lambda publishes a transcoding job message to Amazon SQS, which decouples the upload from processing and handles burst volumes by buffering requests. AWS Elastic Transcoder (or MediaConvert) polls the queue and transcodes videos into multiple formats, storing the output files back to Amazon S3 for durable storage and delivery.

scenarioDescription: A financial services company must store audit logs from all AWS API calls across 15 accounts in an organization. Logs must be immutable, encrypted, centralized in one account, and retained for 7 years to meet regulatory compliance. Any tampering must be detectable.
toolboxServices: AWS CloudTrail|Amazon S3|AWS Organizations|Amazon CloudWatch Logs|AWS Config|S3 Object Lock|S3 Glacier Deep Archive
correctPipelineOrder: AWS CloudTrail|Amazon S3|S3 Object Lock|S3 Glacier Deep Archive
hint: An organization trail captures API activity across all member accounts automatically.
hint2: S3 Object Lock in compliance mode prevents anyone — including the root user — from deleting or modifying log files.
explanation: AWS CloudTrail is configured as an organization trail to capture all API calls across 15 accounts and delivers log files to a centralized Amazon S3 bucket in the audit account. S3 Object Lock in compliance mode makes the logs immutable — preventing deletion or modification by any user including root — with CloudTrail log file integrity validation detecting any tampering. S3 Glacier Deep Archive provides the lowest-cost storage tier for the 7-year retention requirement, with lifecycle policies automatically transitioning logs from S3 Standard.

scenarioDescription: An IoT company collects temperature readings from 100,000 sensors every 5 seconds. The data must be ingested in real-time, transformed (unit conversion, deduplication), and stored in a data lake for batch analytics. A dashboard must show the last 5 minutes of readings in near real-time.
toolboxServices: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams|Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose|AWS Lambda|Amazon S3|Amazon Athena|Amazon Redshift|Amazon DynamoDB
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams|AWS Lambda|Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose|Amazon S3|Amazon Athena
hint: A streaming ingestion service with configurable shard capacity handles high-throughput real-time data from thousands of producers.
hint2: Firehose can deliver transformed data directly to S3 in batches, while Athena queries the data lake using standard SQL without provisioning infrastructure.
explanation: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams ingests the high-throughput sensor data in real-time with configurable shards for the 100K sensor volume. AWS Lambda processes the stream for unit conversion and deduplication as an inline transformation. Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivers the transformed data in micro-batches to Amazon S3, forming the data lake. Amazon Athena provides serverless SQL queries over the S3 data lake for batch analytics and near real-time dashboard queries.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to migrate a 50 TB on-premises Oracle database to Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL with minimal downtime. The application must continue serving traffic during migration, and the schema must be converted from Oracle to PostgreSQL-compatible syntax.
toolboxServices: AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT)|AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)|Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL|AWS Snowball|Amazon RDS for Oracle|AWS DataSync|Amazon Redshift
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT)|AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)|Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL
hint: Before migrating data, the database schema (stored procedures, views, data types) must be converted from Oracle to PostgreSQL syntax.
hint2: DMS supports continuous replication (CDC) that keeps the target in sync while the source remains live, enabling minimal-downtime cutover.
explanation: AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) first converts the Oracle schema — including stored procedures, views, and data types — to PostgreSQL-compatible syntax and applies it to the target. AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) then performs the full data load followed by continuous Change Data Capture (CDC) replication, keeping Aurora in sync with the live Oracle source. Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL serves as the target database, and once replication lag reaches zero, the application cuts over with minimal downtime.

scenarioDescription: A SaaS company needs to isolate tenant data in a shared DynamoDB table where each tenant can only access their own records. The application uses Amazon Cognito for authentication, and the solution must scale to thousands of tenants without creating separate tables or IAM users per tenant.
toolboxServices: Amazon Cognito|AWS IAM|Amazon DynamoDB|AWS STS|Amazon API Gateway|AWS Lambda|Amazon RDS
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon Cognito|AWS STS|AWS IAM|Amazon DynamoDB
hint: Cognito Identity Pool can exchange user tokens for temporary AWS credentials with dynamically scoped IAM policies.
hint2: IAM policy condition keys with ${cognito-identity.amazonaws.com:sub} restrict DynamoDB access to items matching the authenticated user's partition key.
explanation: Amazon Cognito authenticates each tenant user and the Identity Pool uses AWS STS to issue temporary credentials with an assumed IAM role. The AWS IAM policy attached to the role uses a condition key with ${cognito-identity.amazonaws.com:sub} in the dynamodb:LeadingKeys condition, dynamically restricting each tenant to only their own partition key items. Amazon DynamoDB enforces this fine-grained access control at the item level, enabling thousands of tenants to share a single table securely without per-tenant infrastructure.

scenarioDescription: A company runs a critical production workload on EC2 instances behind an ALB. They need to deploy application updates with zero downtime, automatically roll back if health checks fail, and gradually shift traffic from the old version to the new version over 15 minutes.
toolboxServices: AWS CodeDeploy|Elastic Load Balancing (ALB)|Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling|AWS CloudFormation|AWS Elastic Beanstalk|Amazon Route 53|AWS CodePipeline
correctPipelineOrder: AWS CodeDeploy|Elastic Load Balancing (ALB)|Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling
hint: CodeDeploy supports blue/green deployments that provision a new Auto Scaling group and gradually shift ALB traffic.
hint2: The AllowTraffic and BlockTraffic lifecycle hooks combined with linear traffic shifting enable the 15-minute gradual rollout.
explanation: AWS CodeDeploy orchestrates a blue/green deployment — it provisions a new Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group (green) with the updated application, then uses Elastic Load Balancing (ALB) target group switching to gradually shift traffic from the old (blue) to the new (green) fleet over 15 minutes using linear deployment configuration. If ALB health checks detect failures in the green fleet, CodeDeploy automatically rolls back by rerouting all traffic to the original blue fleet, achieving zero-downtime deployments with automatic rollback.

scenarioDescription: A healthcare company must encrypt all data at rest and in transit for HIPAA compliance. They need to manage their own encryption keys with annual automatic rotation, audit all key usage, and ensure that even AWS operators cannot access the plaintext data.
toolboxServices: AWS KMS|AWS CloudHSM|AWS CloudTrail|Amazon S3|AWS Certificate Manager|AWS Secrets Manager|Amazon Macie
correctPipelineOrder: AWS KMS|AWS CloudTrail|Amazon S3
hint: KMS customer-managed keys (CMKs) support automatic annual rotation and integrate natively with most AWS services for encryption at rest.
hint2: Every KMS API call (Encrypt, Decrypt, GenerateDataKey) is logged in CloudTrail, providing a complete audit trail of all key usage.
explanation: AWS KMS creates customer-managed keys (CMKs) with automatic annual rotation enabled, providing envelope encryption for data at rest across AWS services — the key policy ensures only authorized principals can use the keys, and KMS's security boundary prevents even AWS operators from accessing plaintext. AWS CloudTrail logs every KMS API call (Encrypt, Decrypt, GenerateDataKey, etc.), creating a complete audit trail of all key usage for HIPAA compliance auditing. Amazon S3 stores the CloudTrail logs with SSE-KMS encryption, ensuring the audit trail itself is encrypted and tamper-evident.

scenarioDescription: A retail company needs to build a disaster recovery solution for their primary application running in us-east-1. The RTO is 15 minutes and RPO is 1 minute. The application uses an Aurora MySQL database, EC2 instances behind an ALB, and stores user uploads in S3.
toolboxServices: Amazon Aurora Global Database|Amazon Route 53|Amazon S3 Cross-Region Replication|Amazon EC2 AMI Copy|Elastic Load Balancing (ALB)|AWS CloudFormation|AWS Backup
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon Aurora Global Database|Amazon S3 Cross-Region Replication|AWS CloudFormation|Amazon Route 53
hint: Aurora Global Database replicates data to a secondary region with typical lag under 1 second, meeting the 1-minute RPO requirement.
hint2: Route 53 health checks with failover routing automatically redirect traffic to the DR region when the primary becomes unhealthy.
explanation: Amazon Aurora Global Database provides cross-region replication with sub-second lag (meeting the 1-minute RPO), and the secondary cluster can be promoted to read-write in under 1 minute during failover. Amazon S3 Cross-Region Replication keeps user uploads synchronized to the DR region. AWS CloudFormation maintains infrastructure-as-code templates in the DR region that can launch the EC2/ALB stack within minutes (meeting the 15-minute RTO). Amazon Route 53 failover routing with health checks automatically redirects DNS to the DR region's ALB when the primary is unhealthy.

scenarioDescription: A data analytics team needs to run complex SQL queries across petabytes of structured data from multiple sources (sales, inventory, customer). They need columnar storage for fast aggregation queries, automatic scaling, and the ability to query data in their S3 data lake without loading it into the warehouse.
toolboxServices: Amazon Redshift|Amazon Redshift Spectrum|Amazon Athena|Amazon S3|AWS Glue|Amazon RDS|Amazon EMR
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon S3|AWS Glue|Amazon Redshift|Amazon Redshift Spectrum
hint: A data catalog service crawls your S3 data lake to automatically discover schemas and create metadata tables.
hint2: Redshift Spectrum extends your warehouse queries to S3 without loading data, using the Glue Data Catalog as its metastore.
explanation: Data from multiple sources lands in Amazon S3 as the central data lake. AWS Glue crawlers discover the schema and populate the Glue Data Catalog with metadata tables. Amazon Redshift provides the columnar data warehouse for loaded, frequently-queried structured data with automatic scaling (RA3 nodes or Serverless). Amazon Redshift Spectrum extends Redshift SQL queries to the S3 data lake using the Glue Data Catalog, enabling federated queries across warehouse and lake data without ETL loading.

scenarioDescription: A company wants to restrict all S3 buckets in their organization to only allow encrypted uploads, block public access, and ensure no bucket can be created outside of approved AWS regions. These policies must apply automatically to all current and future accounts.
toolboxServices: AWS Organizations SCPs|Amazon S3 Block Public Access|AWS Config|AWS CloudFormation StackSets|AWS IAM|Amazon Macie|AWS Control Tower
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Organizations SCPs|Amazon S3 Block Public Access|AWS Config
hint: Service Control Policies (SCPs) set permission guardrails at the organization level that no IAM policy in member accounts can override.
hint2: AWS Config rules continuously evaluate resource compliance and can auto-remediate non-compliant S3 buckets.
explanation: AWS Organizations SCPs enforce preventive guardrails — denying s3:CreateBucket outside approved regions and denying s3:PutObject without encryption headers — across all member accounts, and no IAM policy can override these restrictions. Amazon S3 Block Public Access is enabled at the organization level to prevent any bucket from being made public. AWS Config rules provide detective controls, continuously evaluating all S3 buckets for compliance (encryption, public access, region) and triggering auto-remediation via Lambda for any non-compliant resources.

scenarioDescription: A gaming company needs to store player session state that must be accessible with sub-millisecond latency. During peak events, read traffic spikes 10x. The data store must automatically scale read capacity without application changes and support automatic cache invalidation when data is updated.
toolboxServices: Amazon DynamoDB|Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX)|Amazon ElastiCache Redis|Amazon RDS|Amazon Aurora|Amazon MemoryDB|Amazon Neptune
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon DynamoDB|Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX)
hint: DynamoDB provides single-digit millisecond latency, but a purpose-built caching layer in front of it delivers sub-millisecond reads.
hint2: DAX is a fully managed, DynamoDB-compatible cache that requires zero application code changes — just point the SDK to the DAX endpoint.
explanation: Amazon DynamoDB stores the player session state with on-demand capacity mode for automatic write scaling. Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) sits in front of DynamoDB as a fully managed, write-through cache that delivers sub-millisecond read latency — it automatically invalidates cached items when the underlying DynamoDB data is updated, and scales read capacity to handle 10x traffic spikes. DAX is API-compatible with DynamoDB, requiring only an endpoint change in the application SDK with zero code modifications.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to connect their on-premises data center to AWS with a dedicated, private 1 Gbps connection for consistent network performance. They also need a backup connection that activates automatically if the primary fails, and all traffic must stay off the public internet.
toolboxServices: AWS Direct Connect|AWS Site-to-Site VPN|AWS Transit Gateway|Amazon VPC|AWS Global Accelerator|Amazon CloudFront|AWS PrivateLink
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Direct Connect|AWS Transit Gateway|AWS Site-to-Site VPN
hint: Direct Connect provides a dedicated physical connection with consistent latency and throughput, unlike internet-based connections.
hint2: A Site-to-Site VPN over the internet serves as an automatic failover backup when the Direct Connect link goes down.
explanation: AWS Direct Connect establishes the primary dedicated 1 Gbps private connection from the on-premises data center to AWS, providing consistent network performance without traversing the public internet. AWS Transit Gateway acts as the central hub connecting the Direct Connect gateway and VPN attachments to multiple VPCs. AWS Site-to-Site VPN provides the encrypted backup connection over the internet that automatically activates via BGP failover when the Direct Connect link fails, ensuring high availability of the hybrid connectivity.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to run a tightly-coupled High Performance Computing (HPC) workload for computational fluid dynamics simulations. The workload requires low-latency inter-node communication, shared POSIX-compliant file storage accessible by all compute nodes, and the ability to scale to 1,000 instances for burst jobs.
toolboxServices: Amazon EC2 (Cluster Placement Group)|Amazon FSx for Lustre|Amazon EFS|Amazon S3|AWS Batch|AWS ParallelCluster|Elastic Fabric Adapter (EFA)
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Batch|Amazon EC2 (Cluster Placement Group)|Elastic Fabric Adapter (EFA)|Amazon FSx for Lustre
hint: A managed batch computing service automatically provisions and scales the optimal compute resources for HPC job queues.
hint2: Elastic Fabric Adapter (EFA) provides OS-bypass networking for MPI-based inter-node communication with latency comparable to on-premises HPC clusters.
explanation: AWS Batch manages the HPC job scheduling, automatically provisioning Amazon EC2 instances in a Cluster Placement Group that co-locates instances on the same underlying hardware for the lowest possible network latency. Elastic Fabric Adapter (EFA) enables OS-bypass networking for the tightly-coupled MPI communication between compute nodes, achieving latency comparable to on-premises InfiniBand clusters. Amazon FSx for Lustre provides the high-performance POSIX-compliant parallel file system that all 1,000 compute nodes can access simultaneously with sub-millisecond latencies and hundreds of GB/s throughput.

scenarioDescription: A company wants to decouple their order processing system. When an order is placed, it must trigger three independent downstream services simultaneously: inventory update, payment processing, and email notification. Each service must process independently and failures in one must not affect the others.
toolboxServices: Amazon SNS|Amazon SQS|AWS Lambda|Amazon EventBridge|Amazon MQ|Amazon Kinesis|AWS Step Functions
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon SNS|Amazon SQS|AWS Lambda
hint: A pub/sub service can fan out a single message to multiple independent subscriber endpoints simultaneously.
hint2: Each downstream service should have its own SQS queue subscription to process independently with isolated failure handling and retry logic.
explanation: Amazon SNS receives the order event and fans it out simultaneously to three Amazon SQS queue subscriptions — one for each downstream service (inventory, payment, email). Each Amazon SQS queue buffers messages independently, ensuring that a failure in one service (e.g., payment) does not block or affect the others, with independent retry policies and dead-letter queues per service. AWS Lambda functions poll each SQS queue to execute the respective business logic, completing the fully decoupled, fault-isolated fan-out architecture.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to host a static single-page application (React) with a custom domain name, HTTPS, and global low-latency delivery. The solution must redirect HTTP to HTTPS, support client-side routing (returning index.html for all paths), and cost as little as possible.
toolboxServices: Amazon S3|Amazon CloudFront|AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)|Amazon Route 53|Amazon EC2|Elastic Load Balancing (ALB)|AWS Amplify
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon Route 53|Amazon CloudFront|AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)|Amazon S3
hint: S3 static website hosting combined with CloudFront provides the lowest-cost architecture for serving static web applications globally.
hint2: ACM provides free public SSL/TLS certificates, but for CloudFront, the certificate must be created in us-east-1 regardless of your primary region.
explanation: Amazon Route 53 resolves the custom domain name with an alias record pointing to the CloudFront distribution. Amazon CloudFront serves as the global CDN with HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect configured in the viewer protocol policy, and a custom error response returning index.html for 403/404 errors to support client-side routing. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) provides a free SSL/TLS certificate (created in us-east-1 for CloudFront). Amazon S3 hosts the static React build files as the CloudFront origin, providing the lowest-cost serverless hosting architecture.

scenarioDescription: A company processes sensitive customer PII data and must ensure that EC2 instances in the private subnet can access S3 and DynamoDB without any traffic traversing the public internet. They also need to access AWS Systems Manager for instance management without public IPs or NAT gateways.
toolboxServices: VPC Gateway Endpoint|VPC Interface Endpoint (PrivateLink)|NAT Gateway|AWS Systems Manager|Amazon S3|Amazon DynamoDB|Internet Gateway
correctPipelineOrder: VPC Gateway Endpoint|VPC Interface Endpoint (PrivateLink)|AWS Systems Manager
hint: S3 and DynamoDB support free Gateway Endpoints that route traffic through the AWS private network via route table entries.
hint2: Systems Manager requires Interface Endpoints (PrivateLink) for ssm, ssmmessages, and ec2messages to work without internet access.
explanation: VPC Gateway Endpoints are created for Amazon S3 and Amazon DynamoDB — these are free, route-table-based endpoints that keep all traffic on the AWS private network without requiring NAT gateways or internet access. VPC Interface Endpoints (PrivateLink) are created for AWS Systems Manager services (ssm, ssmmessages, ec2messages), providing private ENI-based connectivity for instance management. AWS Systems Manager Session Manager then enables secure shell access and patch management for the private EC2 instances without public IPs, NAT gateways, or bastion hosts.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to migrate 200 on-premises virtual machines to AWS within 90 days. They need to discover their current server inventory, analyze dependencies between servers, generate a migration plan, and perform the actual lift-and-shift rehosting with minimal downtime.
toolboxServices: AWS Application Discovery Service|AWS Migration Hub|AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)|AWS Server Migration Service|Amazon EC2|AWS DataSync|AWS Transfer Family
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Application Discovery Service|AWS Migration Hub|AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)|Amazon EC2
hint: Before migrating, you need to discover what servers exist and map their network dependencies to plan migration waves.
hint2: Application Migration Service (MGN) performs continuous block-level replication, enabling cutover with minutes of downtime.
explanation: AWS Application Discovery Service deploys agents on the 200 on-premises VMs to collect server specifications, performance data, and network dependency mappings. AWS Migration Hub aggregates the discovery data and provides a central dashboard to plan migration waves based on application dependencies and prioritization. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN) performs continuous block-level replication of each server to AWS, enabling non-disruptive testing and final cutover with minutes of downtime. The migrated workloads run on Amazon EC2 instances in the target AWS environment.

scenarioDescription: A company runs a microservices application on Amazon ECS Fargate. They need to implement service-to-service authentication, encrypt all inter-service traffic with mutual TLS, control traffic routing for canary deployments, and gain observability into service communication without modifying application code.
toolboxServices: AWS App Mesh|Amazon ECS Fargate|AWS X-Ray|Amazon Route 53|Elastic Load Balancing (ALB)|AWS Cloud Map|Amazon API Gateway
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Cloud Map|AWS App Mesh|Amazon ECS Fargate|AWS X-Ray
hint: A service mesh provides sidecar proxies (Envoy) that handle mTLS, traffic routing, and observability transparently without application code changes.
hint2: Cloud Map provides service discovery that App Mesh uses to locate and route traffic between microservices dynamically.
explanation: AWS Cloud Map provides service discovery, registering each microservice's endpoints so App Mesh can dynamically locate services. AWS App Mesh deploys Envoy sidecar proxies alongside each Amazon ECS Fargate task, transparently handling mutual TLS encryption, service-to-service authentication, and weighted traffic routing for canary deployments — all without modifying application code. AWS X-Ray integrates with the App Mesh Envoy proxies to provide distributed tracing and observability into all service-to-service communication patterns, latencies, and error rates.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to build a data lake that ingests raw data from multiple sources (databases, SaaS applications, streaming), catalogs the data automatically, transforms it into analytics-ready formats (Parquet), and makes it queryable via standard SQL without managing any servers.
toolboxServices: Amazon S3|AWS Glue|AWS Lake Formation|Amazon Athena|Amazon Redshift|Amazon EMR|AWS Data Pipeline
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Lake Formation|Amazon S3|AWS Glue|Amazon Athena
hint: Lake Formation simplifies data lake setup by centralizing permissions, data ingestion, and catalog management in a single service.
hint2: Glue ETL jobs can convert raw CSV/JSON data into columnar Parquet format, dramatically improving Athena query performance and reducing costs.
explanation: AWS Lake Formation orchestrates the data lake creation — configuring S3 storage, setting up fine-grained access controls, and ingesting data from multiple sources (databases via JDBC, streaming, SaaS connectors). Amazon S3 serves as the durable, scalable storage layer for the data lake. AWS Glue crawlers automatically discover schemas and populate the Data Catalog, while Glue ETL jobs transform raw data into optimized Parquet format. Amazon Athena provides serverless SQL queries directly over the S3 data lake using the Glue Data Catalog, with no infrastructure to manage.
